pullRequest.title: Implement two-tiered Ctrl+C handling for graceful session navigation
pullRequest.body:
Description

This PR implements a two-tiered Ctrl+C handling mechanism as requested in #474. The new behavior allows users to gracefully navigate between the command prompt and session selection menu without terminating Pacu entirely.

New Behavior

At the Pacu command prompt:

  • First Ctrl+C: Displays hint message: [*] Press Ctrl+C again within 2 seconds to return to session menu.
  • Second Ctrl+C (within 2 seconds): Returns to session selection menu
  • Timeout: If more than 2 seconds pass, the counter resets

During module execution:

  • Ctrl+C: Interrupts the running module and returns to the command prompt with message: [!] Module <name> interrupted by user (Ctrl+C). Returning to Pacu prompt...

At the session selection menu:

  • Ctrl+C: Exits Pacu entirely (unchanged behavior)

Changes Made

  1. Added interrupt tracking infrastructure (__init__ method):

    • INTERRUPT_TIMEOUT = 2.0 class constant
    • _interrupt_count, _last_interrupt_time, _module_running instance variables
    • _reset_interrupt_state() and _record_interrupt() helper methods

  2. Modified exec_module():

    • Updated SystemExit handler to display user-friendly message when module is interrupted

  3. Rewrote idle() method:

    • Now returns bool indicating whether to return to session menu
    • Implements two-tier Ctrl+C detection with timeout
    • Properly distinguishes between SIGINT (Ctrl+C) and exit command

  4. Modified run_gui():

    • Handles idle() return value to loop back to session selection when appropriate

  5. Changed exit() method:

    • Uses 'Pacu exit' instead of 'SIGINT called' to distinguish exit command from Ctrl+C

  6. Updated help text:

    • Added "Keyboard Shortcuts" section documenting the new Ctrl+C behavior

Testing Performed

Test Case | Expected Result | Status -- | -- | -- Single Ctrl+C at prompt | Shows hint message | ✅ Double Ctrl+C at prompt (within 2s) | Returns to session menu | ✅ Ctrl+C, wait >2s, Ctrl+C | Shows hint again (counter reset) | ✅ Ctrl+C during module execution | Returns to prompt with message | ✅ exit command | Exits Pacu with "Bye!" | ✅ quit command | Exits Pacu with "Bye!" | ✅ Session switching after double Ctrl+C | Can select different session | ✅ Ctrl+C at session selection menu | Exits Pacu entirely | ✅

Technical Notes

  • Pacu uses a custom signal handler (set_sigint_handler) that converts SIGINT to SystemExit('SIGINT called') rather than raising KeyboardInterrupt. The implementation accounts for this.
  • The interrupt counter is reset after each successful command execution to prevent accidental session exits.
  • EOF (Ctrl+D) handling is preserved and returns to session selection.

Fixes #474

Checklist

  • [x] Targeted at master branch
  • [x] Descriptive title
  • [x] Description references related issue (#474)
  • [x] Manual testing completed
  • [x] Code follows Python style conventions

pullRequest.title: fix: false positive success message in iam__privesc_scan
pullRequest.body:
Closes #483

`iam__privesc_scan` reports "Privilege escalation was successful" even
when every method fails. The check `if response is False` uses identity
comparison, so if an escalation method returns `None` (e.g. falling through
a try/except without an explicit return), `None is False` evaluates to
`False` and the code treats it as a success.

Fix: change `if response is False` to `if not response` so that `None`,
`False`, and any other falsy return value is correctly treated as failure.

pullRequest.title: fix: derive region from bucket in cfn__resource_injection (closes #484)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Resolves region being hardcoded to `us-east-1` in `cfn__resource_injection`, causing `ResourceNotFoundException` when the target bucket is in any other region
- Region is now resolved once from the bucket name immediately after `bucket` is known, and passed explicitly to `attacker_sess`, `lambda_env`, and `s3_notifications_sess`
- Also updates `mypy` from `^0.812` to `^1.0` to fix `typed-ast` build failure on Python 3.11+

Supersedes #497.

pullRequest.title: fix: resolve --version returning unknown in Docker
pullRequest.body:
Closes #486

`get_pacu_version()` reads `pyproject.toml` by walking up from `pacu/main.py`.
After `pip install .` (as the Dockerfile does), the code runs from site-packages
where `pyproject.toml` doesn't exist, so it falls back to "unknown".

Fix: use `importlib.metadata.version()` (stdlib since Python 3.8) as the primary
source. This reads from installed package metadata, which `pip install` populates
correctly. The `pyproject.toml` fallback stays for development installs.

pullRequest.title: feat: improve delete_session with multi-select and file cleanup
pullRequest.body:
- Allow selecting multiple sessions at once (e.g. "1 3 5")
- Prompt whether to also delete the session output files from the filesystem
- Skip active session with a warning instead of aborting entirely
- Update help text to reflect new behavior

Closes #488
Supersedes #490
